title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
summary: Cursor's Sam Whitmore, Baseten's Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge discuss scaling agent workflows to run hundreds of agents concurrently with inter-agent communication and collaborative code review. The focus is on moving from task parallelization toward cohesive multi-agent systems design.
writeup: Cursor's Sam Whitmore talks with Baseten leaders about running and coordinating large numbers of coding agents simultaneously. The discussion covers operating agents at scale, agent-to-agent communication and review, and a shift from parallelizing individual tasks toward system-level design. The framing connects agent orchestration, test-time compute, and multi-agent systems as the structure for that coordination.
